findings.description: The function _write is never called with a valid Path object, which could lead to unexpected behavior.
findings.evidence:
def _write(tmp_path: Path, name: str, content: str) -> Path:
    p = tmp_path / name
    p.write_text(textwrap.dedent(content), encoding="utf-8")
    return p

findings.description: The function _python_signatures is not handling the case where the input path is None.
The function _python_imports is not handling the case where the input path is None.

implicit_assumptions.assumption: The function _write is only called with a valid Path object, but the Path object is not validated for existence.
implicit_assumptions.risk: the function may write to an unexpected location
